Justification: Primary users: Individuals with mobility impairments (spinal cord injuries, muscular dystrophy, cerebral palsy, multiple sclerosis, age-related decline).; Users with cognitive impairments: Accommodated via simplified controls, tactile feedback, pre-programmed routes, and intuitive interfaces. Design adaptations validated through user testing with diverse cognitive spectrums.; Anthropometrics: Max user weight 150 kg. Design optimizes for user independence, minimizing complex maneuvers or external assistance needs.; User testing focus: Refining interface accessibility, safety alert clarity, and physical control ergonomics for users with cognitive impairments.

Mobility: Reliable on smooth indoor floors, paved outdoor pathways, and moderately uneven terrain (well-maintained grass, fine to medium gravel).; Incline: Capable of ascending 10-degree sustained inclines at a minimum speed of 3 km/h with maximum load.; Turning Radius: Minimum 'on-the-spot' turning radius < 1.5 meters for maneuverability.; Obstacle Clearance: 5cm clearance achieved via robust wheel/suspension using specified high-strength, recyclable aluminum alloys (e.g., 6061-T6 with >50% post-consumer recycled content, verified quality control). Feasibility confirmed during detailed design. Stability during turns will be ensured.; Ground Clearance: Minimum 12 cm under chassis and critical components.

Range: Minimum 25-30 km on a single charge (tested: flat terrain, 75kg user, 6 km/h).; Battery Technology: Lithium Iron Phosphate (LiFePO4) with a targeted lifespan of at least 1000 charge cycles. End-of-life plan details logistics, recycling partnerships (e.g., with certified Li-ion recyclers), and target material recovery rates (>80%).; Charging: Full charge time ≤ 8 hours. Charging system efficiency >90%.; Battery Management System (BMS): Includes safety features and facilitates safe, easy, tool-less removal for service, reuse, refurbishment, second-life applications, and recycling. Battery packs designed for secure removal with clear handling instructions.; Battery Pack Design: Facilitates reuse, refurbishment, second-life applications, and recycling. Charging system designed for repairability and longevity.

Seating Adjustments: Standardized ranges (lumbar, seat depth, backrest angle) using mechanisms reducing part complexity. Materials selected for easy separation for recycling, minimizing glues/adhesives.; Cushioning: High-density recycled foam and durable, washable, sustainably sourced natural fibers (e.g., hemp, organic cotton, certified). Materials processed for easy separability.; Modular Components: Armrests adjustable height/width, designed as modular components for customization/replacement. Footrests: eco-friendly, non-slip, adjustable height/angle.; Controls: Ergonomic joystick (left/right option), adjustable sensitivity, tactile feedback on all controls. Validated with users of varying dexterity and sensory perception.; Vibration/Noise: Minimized through material selection and design, ensuring pleasant experience without compromising recyclability. Seat cushion materials durable and washable.

Braking System: Robust electromagnetic and manual parking brakes. All safety components designed for easy replacement, repair, and recyclability, using specified materials (e.g., recycled polyester for harnesses).; Anti-Tip Wheels: Standard, effective on moderate inclines and obstacles.; Seatbelts/Harnesses: Made from sustainable materials (e.g., recycled polyester), adjustable, easy-to-use, designed for users with limited dexterity. Robust and easily replaceable/recyclable.; Lighting: Integrated LED lighting (front/rear) using responsibly sourced, recyclable materials.; Audible Indicator: Horn included for alerts.; Emergency Stop: Clearly defined, integrated with motor control, robust, easily replaceable module, designed for end-of-life management.; Regulatory Compliance: Meets ISO 7176 series, FDA regulations. Compliance processes integrated from outset. Materials selected to minimize WEEE challenges.

Frame/Structure: Recycled aluminum alloys (e.g., 6061-T6, >50% post-consumer recycled content, stringent QC for consistent performance/fatigue resistance). Avoids composite structures for structural elements.; Component Lifespans: Defined for high-wear items (tires, joysticks, motors). Prioritizes mono-materials where possible.; Sealing: Sealed bearings and motor enclosures use recyclable materials with minimal hazardous substances.; Modular Connections: Modules connected using durable, reusable non-permanent fasteners (screws, clips) and minimal adhesives, avoiding glued assemblies. Connection points designed for easy replacement over 10+ year lifespan.

Recycled Content Mandate: >50% recycled aluminum (frame), >70% recycled plastic (non-structural). Low embodied energy materials prioritized. Material Passports detail composition, origin, recyclability.; Energy Efficiency: Target specific energy consumption metrics (e.g., <15 Wh/km). Regenerative braking incorporated.; Design for Disassembly (DFD): Specific fastener types (screws, clips), avoidance of permanent adhesives, mono-materials for easy recovery. Recycling pathways defined for complex assemblies (motors, electronics, batteries) with target recovery rates (>80%).; Finishes/Packaging: Low-VOC or water-based paints. Minimized packaging (recycled/recyclable, easy separation).; Product Lifespan: Target 10+ years. Designed for repairability and upgradeability. Spare parts guaranteed for 10+ years post-production. LCA or equivalent criteria document developed.

Aesthetic: Modern, sleek, unobtrusive design promoting user dignity. Low-VOC/water-based paints/coatings.; Customization: Muted natural tones, customizable accents from easily separable, recyclable materials. Design prioritizes intuitive operation, ease of cleaning, and maintenance.; User Interface: Intuitive, clear labeling, visual feedback. Customizable accents do not compromise main structure recyclability.; User Experience: Positive and empowering, considering ease of disassembly for repair/recycling.; Product Perception: Avoids 'medical' connotations, aiming for user pride.

Tools/Parts: Standard tools required. Replacement parts made from recycled content where feasible, designed for easy remanufacturing/reuse. Spare parts guaranteed for 10+ years.; Disassembly: Design principles using non-permanent fasteners (screws, clips), minimal adhesives. Components easily accessible. Connection points designed for repeated use.; Manuals/Diagnostics: User/service manuals detail maintenance, troubleshooting. Diagnostics provide specific component failure data for targeted repair, accessible to users for basic troubleshooting.; Efficiency: Integrated diagnostics reduce unnecessary service calls. Modular design allows quick replacement of wear items, minimizing downtime.; Repairability: Components designed for easy repair, remanufacturing, or direct reuse.

Target Retail Price: Competitive within accessible market ($3,000-$5,000 USD range). Cost-saving measures do not compromise durability, repairability, or recyclability.; Material/Manufacturing: Optimized for cost while prioritizing sustainability and circularity. Lifecycle cost analysis incorporates environmental externalities.; Long-Term Cost: Emphasized lower costs due to durability, repairability, energy efficiency. Upfront cost does not compromise circularity principles.; Modularity: Supports tiered pricing with sustainable features in all tiers.; Subsidies/Reimbursement: Design choices support certifications for insurance/governmental subsidies. Cost considerations align with lifecycle and circularity goals.

Standards: ISO 7176 series, FDA, IEC 60601-1-2 (EMC), WEEE, and relevant national regulations. Materials selected to minimize WEEE challenges.; End-of-Life Management: Design facilitates compliance with battery disposal and de-manufacturing regulations. Battery packs easily identifiable for collection/recycling.; Certifications: CE marking, FDA clearance pursued as required. Processes, timelines, and costs integrated from project outset.; Labeling/Documentation: Clear, compliant labeling and documentation, including end-of-life management directives.

Modularity: Optional features designed as modular add-ons, easily separable and recyclable. Standard interfaces for components robust for mobility device use.; Functionality: Smartphone integration (Bluetooth/Wi-Fi) for diagnostics, monitoring, personalized settings. Low-power modes, user control over activation to minimize energy consumption.; Safety/Privacy: GPS tracking considered; components easy to replace/recycle, avoiding proprietary connectors. Data privacy and security addressed.; Longevity: Roadmap for software updates and compatibility ensures product longevity. Design allows future upgrades without complete redesign.
